title: People applying for probate
question: Do you want to apply with anyone else?
questionParent: Do you want to make a joint application with {deceasedName}’s other parent?
paragraph1: If there are other people entitled to inherit, you can make a joint application with up to 3 of them. They will also be named on the grant of probate.
paragraph2: All applicants will be given the legal right to deal with {deceasedName}’s estate. This includes:
paragraph1Parent: You can make a joint application with {deceasedName}’s other parent.
paragraph2Parent: They will also be named on the grant of probate and will be given the legal right to deal with {deceasedName}’s estate. This includes:
paragraph1Spouse: Based on the information you have given us, there are other people entitled to inherit who you can apply with.
paragraph2Spouse: You can only make a joint application with {deceasedName}’s children, or, if any of the children died before {deceasedName}, their children ({deceasedName}’s grandchildren).
bullet1: collecting the estate’s assets (property, money and possessions)
bullet3: distributing what is left to the people entitled to it
paragraph3: Not applying does not affect a person’s entitlement to inherit.
paragraph3Parent: Not applying does not affect their entitlement to inherit.
paragraph4Spouse: If you want to make a joint application, you will have to apply by post using Form PA1A (opens in a new tab) instead.

hasCoApplicant.required: Select ‘Yes’ if you want to apply with another person
hasCoApplicant.invalid: You can only apply with up to 3 other people
hasCoApplicant.requiredParent: Select ‘Yes’ if you want to apply together with {deceasedName}’s other parent
hasCoApplicant.requiredSpouse: Select ‘Yes’ if you want to apply together with a child or grandchild of {deceasedName}
